summary: free-anthropic-claude is an Anthropic SDK impersonator that drops a Python crypto-wallet stealer at install time
details: Package masquerades as an Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper (package.json description claims 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper') but ships no Anthropic API surface — the README and code describe an unrelated 'cachesync-helper' cache library. The package.json postinstall hook runs `node lib/cli.js`, which invokes a `_prefetch()` routine in lib/index.js that POSTs to four hardcoded IPv4 endpoints (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146:8443) with TLS verification disabled (`rejectUnauthorized: false`), base64-decodes the response into files on disk, and spawns `python3 main.py` detached. To guarantee execution, the loader bootstraps a Python runtime when one is missing or too new: `winget` on Windows, `brew` on macOS, and a Miniconda3 download from repo.anaconda.com on Linux, then pip-installs `bip-utils`, `mnemonic`, `eth-account`, `pycryptodome`, and `psutil` — the canonical dependency set of a crypto-wallet/seed-phrase stealer. On macOS, the code additionally writes `INSERT OR REPLACE` statements directly into `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db` to grant `kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles` and `kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData` to Terminal, the running node binary, and /usr/bin/python3 — a privacy-consent-bypass technique that hands the dropped payload full-disk access. All sensitive API names are reconstructed via array-join obfuscation (`['constr','uctor'].join('')`, `['child','_pro','cess'].join('')`, `['htt','ps'].join('')`, `['ba','se','64'].join('')`, `['py','thon','3'].join('')`) to evade static detection of `require('child_process')`, https, base64, and python3. Installing this package fully compromises the developer's machine: arbitrary attacker-controlled Python code is fetched and executed at install time with elevated macOS privacy grants, and crypto-wallet/seed-phrase extraction tooling is staged on the host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
